role: This file is the central token-driven visual stylesheet for the Surface interface, encoding layout chrome, component cosmetics, responsive form-factor adaptations, and overlay/modal presentation for the wheel-based explorer.
capabilities: Token-only styling via CSS custom properties (spacing, radii, typography, ink/pigment colors)
Three authored responsive layouts: desktop, portrait, and landscape
Top/bottom chrome bars and one-glance title block
Three-column stage grid with left/right strata and center wheel host
Portrait wheel-region square constraint and optical centering
Loading/breathing state indicator
Disclosure card variants: absolute panel, fixed bottom sheet, and rail
Selection tether overlay styling (dotted curve, node, opacity transition)
Lens chip dropdown menu styling with right-edge collision anchor class
Active relative-centre chip styling
View-mode toggle (circle/square coordinate systems)
Settings gear popover scaffold
Live/freeze indicator with pulsing animation
Time scrubber bar with present/past visual states
Verification-mode banner styling
Notice/toast banner styling
Decisions inbox entry pill and modal list styling
Tools palette entry, modal, list, detail, and schema-driven form engine styling
Scale rung ladder for units/themes
Connection readout for drive-to-explore
Collapsible legend for lens orientation
Two-gravity separator overlays (pole labels, counts, verdict, reset)
Type-nucleation overlays and picker controls
Gallery face overlay for drilled-unit modal with phone-archetype width
Form-factor allocation via body[data-ff] classes
Reduced-motion guard for live indicator pulse
Desktop panel max-width media query
substance_why: The file contains hundreds of detailed selectors, extensive design-rationale comments, three responsive layouts, multiple modal/sheet/overlay systems, a form-engine skin, and host-specific bridge rules for DNA/fork integration.
interactions_present: Hover, active, and focus-visible visual feedback on buttons, pills, chips, rows, and toggles
CSS opacity/visibility transitions for inbox, tools, gallery, and tether overlays
Open/closed overlay state styling via modifier classes
Toggle on/off state styling for pills, view buttons, live dot, and form pills
Legend collapse styling driven by data-collapsed attribute
Range input accent-color theming for scrubber and nucleation dials
Chrome visibility suppression when gallery modal is open
Reduced-motion media query disabling live-pulse animation
interactions_missing: No JavaScript event wiring for opening/closing inbox, tools, gallery, settings, or lens menus
No keyboard focus trap, Escape handling, or arrow navigation for modals/sheets/menus
No scroll lock or body overflow suppression when overlays are open
No touch swipe/drag handling for bottom sheets
No ARIA roles, aria-expanded, aria-hidden, or live-region attributes
No actual tether geometry/opacity updates (relies on Tether.tsx rAF loop)
No actual chip menu collision detection (relies on useChipMenu measurements)
No actual settings panel top positioning (relies on Settings.tsx inline style)
No actual form validation, submission, or dynamic error-state wiring
No actual live/freeze toggle state change or scrubber input handling
No actual verify-mode URL detection logic
No actual decision deep-link resolution or error trigger logic
No reduced-motion guard for the breathing animation
No dark-mode, high-contrast, or print media queries
performance_risks: Infinite CSS animations (.breathing and .livedot--on live-pulse) may cause continuous compositor work and battery drain
Full-viewport fixed tether overlay with rAF-driven updates and opacity transitions can trigger frequent repaints
Many fixed/absolute overlays and high z-index layers create large stacking contexts and composite layers
Heavy use of box-shadows, color-mix, and transitions across many interactive elements without will-change hints
No containment or will-change on animated elements, risking paint/layout thrash on hover transforms
Complex selector overrides for DNA host integration increase style recalculation cost
reuse_why: The file encodes the intended visual grammar, token discipline, and responsive allocation in one place; keep the token/layout core and component contracts, but refactor into scoped stylesheets and remove host-bridge hacks once DNA/fork seams finalize.
decomposition_hints: Wheel visualization chrome (.wheel-region, .rungladder, .coincidence, .theme-label, separators, nucleation) → wheel.css
Disclosure, tether, and detail card (.disclosure, .tether-*, .disc-*) → inspector.css
Tools palette and form engine (.tools-*, .tf-*) → tools.css
Gallery overlay and DNA host overrides (.gallery-*) → gallery.css
Theme tokens remain in paper.css (already separate)
